question: Explain the design data and operating conditions for the secondary side of the steam generator (SG).

nuscale_ch05_0019.title: Reactor Coolant System Component and Subsystem Design
nuscale_ch05_0019.summary: This section describes the design of the NuScale Power Module’s Reactor Coolant System (RCS) components and subsystems, including the reactor pressure vessel, steam generators, safety and emergency valves, and associated piping. It details the integration of these components within the reactor vessel, outlines their boundaries between primary and secondary systems, and references where further design information can be found in other report sections.

nuscale_ch05_0025.title: References
nuscale_ch05_0025.summary: This section details the materials used in the construction of the pressurizer and its components in the NuScale reactor, specifying alloys and standards for each part to ensure corrosion resistance and compatibility with operating environments. It references relevant sections of the BPVC (Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code) and lists the applicable material specifications for components such as the baffle plate, heater bundles, cover plate, and bolting.

nuscale_ch05_0017.title: Reactor Vessel Integrity
nuscale_ch05_0017.summary: This section outlines the procedures and regulatory compliance measures to ensure the integrity of the NuScale reactor vessel over its 60-year design life. It details the development of operating procedures to maintain pressure-temperature limits, evaluates the vessel’s resistance to pressurized thermal shock and upper-shelf energy degradation, and confirms that predicted material properties remain within regulatory safety criteria throughout the vessel’s operational lifespan.

nuscale_ch05_0016.title: Pressure-Temperature Limits, Pressurized Thermal Shock, and Charpy Upper-Shelf Energy Data and Analyses
nuscale_ch05_0016.summary: This section outlines the basis for establishing operational pressure and temperature limits for the reactor coolant pressure boundary (RCPB) to comply with regulatory requirements over the plant’s 60-year lifespan. It describes the use of ASME and NRC methodologies to calculate pressure-temperature limit curves, accounting for vessel embrittlement, and provides figures and tables with the applicable limits to ensure protection against non-ductile failure.

nuscale_ch05_0020.title: Steam Generators
nuscale_ch05_0020.summary: The steam generators (SGs) in the NuScale Power Module are integrated within the reactor pressure vessel (RPV), which also houses other key components such as the pressurizer and safety valves. The SG system includes feedwater and main steam piping, plenum access ports, and thermal relief valves, with the RPV forming the outer pressure boundary and the SG tubes and welds contributing to the reactor coolant pressure boundary.

nuscale_ch05_0006.title: System Evaluation
nuscale_ch05_0006.summary: This section describes the evaluation of the NuScale Power Plant’s system performance, focusing on optimizing steady-state parameters such as coolant temperatures, flow rates, and pressure to maximize electrical generation and maintain safety margins. Calculations using NRELAP5 software, supported by hand calculations, determine best-estimate, maximum, and minimum flow conditions while accounting for uncertainties and ensuring low pressure losses to support natural circulation in the reactor coolant system.

nuscale_ch05_0009.title: Overpressure Protection
nuscale_ch05_0009.summary: This section outlines NuScale's compliance with regulatory requirements for overpressure protection, referencing specific ASME Boiler and Pressure Vessel Code (BPVC) and Operations and Maintenance (OM) code cases as mandated by 10 CFR 50.55a and related Regulatory Guides (RGs). It details how code cases for design, fabrication, construction, preservice, and inservice inspections and testing are selected and verified to meet NRC conditions, with a consolidated list provided in Table 5.2-1.

nuscale_ch05_0021.title: Reactor Coolant System Piping
nuscale_ch05_0021.summary: The section discusses the design and degradation assessment of the NuScale reactor coolant system's steam generator (SG) tubing. It notes that the primary degradation mechanism observed in similar operating reactors is wear from flow-induced vibration or foreign objects, and that the NuScale design—with its helical tubing and novel support structure—does not increase risk compared to existing designs. Prototypic testing and standard SG program guidelines are deemed sufficient, with wear remaining the most likely issue and corrosion risks assessed as low, supporting a 40% through-wall defect plugging criterion.

nuscale_ch05_0005.title: System Components
nuscale_ch05_0005.summary: This section describes key components of the NuScale reactor system. It details the Reactor Pressure Vessel (RPV), which houses the reactor core and supports major systems, and outlines the main Reactor Coolant System (RCS) piping and its connections. The section also briefly introduces the pressurizer (PZR), which is integrated into the RPV.

edges.description: Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, while Section B gives detailed material specifications for the pressurizer, which is one of the components mentioned in Section A.

Section A describes the components and design basis of the reactor coolant system, including the steam generators, while Section B addresses the development of operating procedures to ensure that pressure-temperature limits (which are influenced by the design and operation of components like the steam generator) are not exceeded during operation.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, while Section B details specific material and weld requirements for reactor vessel attachments, but neither section provides direct design data or operating conditions for the secondary side of the steam generator, so their relationship is limited to both referencing components within the reactor vessel.

Section A provides a general overview of the reactor coolant system components, including the steam generators, while Section B focuses specifically on the steam generators as one of those components, thus Section B specifies details for the general description in Section A.

Section B discusses the establishment of steady state values for secondary side parameters of the steam generator as a function of reactor power, which are the design data and operating conditions that Section A's general description of the steam generator components refers to.

Section A describes the components and subsystems of the reactor coolant system, including the steam generators, while Section B outlines the overpressure protection and applicable ASME code requirements that govern the design and construction of these components, thus connecting the design data in A to the regulatory compliance in B.

Section A references material specifications and component descriptions for the pressurizer, while Section B discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, both of which are prerequisites for establishing safe design data and operating conditions for the secondary side of the steam generator.
Section A references Section B by directing the reader to Section 5.2 for material descriptions of the RPV upper shell and upper head, which are relevant to the pressurizer's design.

Section A describes the pressurizer components and materials within the reactor pressure vessel, while Section B provides an overview of the reactor pressure vessel's internal components, including both the pressurizer and the steam generators, thus structurally connecting the pressurizer (A) as one of the components listed in the broader system context (B).

Section A describes specific pressurizer components and their materials, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that these components must comply with for design and fabrication.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, which are based on the technical requirements and analyses described in Section B.

Section A discusses operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, which is relevant to Section B because the steam generators (in Section B) are components within the reactor vessel whose design and operation must comply with these limits.

Section B establishes steady state operating parameters for the secondary side of the steam generator, which must be maintained within the pressure-temperature limits and operating procedures described in Section A to ensure reactor vessel integrity.

Section A discusses the development of operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded, while Section B outlines compliance with ASME and NRC overpressure protection codes, both addressing how design and operational controls maintain the integrity of the reactor coolant boundary under specified conditions.

Section A discusses pressure-temperature limits and material properties for the reactor vessel, while Section B describes the inclusion of steam generators within the reactor pressure vessel, connecting them through shared design and operating conditions relevant to the analysis question.

Section A discusses ASME BPVC code requirements for reactor vessel attachments and welds, while Section B addresses compliance with ASME BPVC and regulatory provisions for overpressure protection, both referencing ASME BPVC standards as the connecting information.

Section B establishes the steady state values and performance criteria for the secondary side of the steam generator, while Section A provides the design details and components relevant to the steam generator, thus Section A specifies the components that Section B evaluates for operating conditions.

Section A describes the components and design basis of the steam generators, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that the design and construction of these components, including the steam generators, must comply with.

Section A discusses the establishment of steady state parameters for the secondary side of the steam generator to optimize plant performance, while Section B outlines the regulatory codes and standards (ASME BPVC, 10 CFR 50.55a) that these design and operating conditions must comply with, connecting the design data to regulatory requirements.

Section A provides specific information about SG tube degradation mechanisms and risk assessment, which elaborates on the general description of steam generator components and their design context introduced in Section B.

Section A discusses observed degradation mechanisms and design considerations for steam generator tubing, while Section B addresses the development of operating procedures to ensure pressure-temperature limits are not exceeded during operation; both sections are related through their focus on maintaining safe operating conditions and integrity of reactor components under specified design and operational parameters.

Section A describes operational procedures and water chemistry conditions for the secondary side of the steam generator, while Section B provides the overall design and configuration of the reactor coolant system, including the steam generators, thus Section A specifies operational details for the components generally described in Section B.

Section A references specific system components and operational steps that involve the pressurizer, while Section B provides detailed material specifications and component descriptions for the pressurizer mentioned in Section A.

Section A describes the operational steps and water chemistry conditions for the secondary side of the steam generator, which are part of the design data and operating conditions that Section B requires to be controlled through operating procedures to ensure reactor vessel integrity.

Section A discusses SG tube degradation mechanisms and design considerations, while Section B outlines operational procedures and secondary water chemistry management, both providing information relevant to the design data and operating conditions of the SG secondary side.
